subject: Recap: Hands-on with Anthropic's Fable 5
body:
Hello First Name,

This week's 🦄 AI That Works session dove into "Hands-on with Fable 5: Exploring Anthropic's New Model."

The full recording, code, and diagrams from the session are now available on GitHub:
https://github.com/hellovai/ai-that-works

We spent a good chunk of time getting familiar with Anthropic's new Fable 5 model, putting it to the test on some tough engineering problems. Here's a quick rundown:

*   We challenged Fable 5 with some of our most difficult engineering problems, including a design document for the BAML VM's observability layer and a complex race-condition benchmark.
*   It actually gave us some valuable insights. For example, it suggested ways to optimize data structures for high-throughput observability and even pointed out minor architectural inconsistencies.
*   However, it also overlooked a few key architectural details. These included the need for U64 thread IDs when spawning threads at high rates and the right correlation key for a tricky race condition.

Our main takeaway from this session: New models often come with a lot of buzz, but real value usually comes from testing them on your toughest, most specific problems. Even small improvements in how they handle constraints can make a big difference in complex engineering work. It's all about finding where they truly shine by pushing their limits.

Our next session tomorrow will be all about "Agent Observability." This was originally planned for this week.

If you have any questions, just reply to this email or hop into our Discord: https://www.boundaryml.com/discord. We're always happy to chat! Happy coding 🧑‍💻

Vaibhav & Dex
call_to_action: Ask questions on Discord or reply to this email.
